name: Dreamland (Chinese: 梦幻沙滩; Indonesian: Pantai Dreamland / Pantai New Kuta Beach)
distance_from_dps_airport_km: Approximately 16-18 km from Ngurah Rai (DPS) Airport (around 35-45 min by car)
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~70-85 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: ~10-15 min by scooter
wave_type: A-frame beach break / reef break with sandy and flat-rock-shelf bottom; short right and longer fast left
skill_level: Beginner to Intermediate primary; Advanced on bigger days (4 ft+) when outside peaks fire
wave_height_range: 2-6 ft; average 2-5 ft; most consistent and fun at 3-5 ft
bottom_type: Mixed - sand on the inside (forgiving) with flat rock/reef shelf underneath; deeper water when small, shallower feel when big
best_season: Dry season, May to September/October (peak June-August); SW-W groundswells with E trade winds
best_tide: Low to mid tide (lower is better); avoid full high tide which can backwash off the cliff/shorebreak
best_wind: Offshore from the East (E trades); the cliff/river gap funnels cross-offshore winds into directly offshore
swell_consistency_rating: 4/5 during dry season - reliable but smaller than Uluwatu/Padang [uncertain]
wave_length_meters: Short to medium - typically 30-80 m rides; left section can run longer on bigger days
wave_speed_rating: Medium - mellower than Bukit reef breaks; left can be fast on larger days
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Low to moderate - forgiving drop with sandy/deeper take-off; outside peaks on bigger swells have steeper drops
secondary_peaks: Yes - inside left (beginner-friendly), outside A-frame (left and right), occasional smaller peaks along the bay
peak_crowd_level: 3/5 - moderate; busier in beginner lesson hours 9am-12pm and weekends; less crowded than Padang/Uluwatu
access: Easy - drive in via Jalan New Kuta Raya through the New Kuta/Pecatu Indah Resort area, paved road to large parking lot, then short flight of stairs/paved path down to beach (~5 min walk)
paddle_out_method: Paddle straight out from the sand through the shorebreak; use the channel between the A-frame peaks; minimal duck-diving required on small days
entry_fee: Parking IDR 5,000-10,000 (scooter/car); informal beach donation ~IDR 10,000 sometimes collected by locals; access through New Kuta Resort is generally free
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (6-8am) for glassy and uncrowded; second window 4-6pm for sunset session
best_time_of_day: Early morning before lessons start (~7am) or late afternoon after 3pm
nearby_warung: Beachfront warungs serving nasi goreng, fresh coconuts, Bintang; Klapa New Kuta Beach Club above the cliff; food stalls in the parking area; Jenggala Café nearby
alternative_spot_within_10min: Balangan (left point, more advanced), Bingin (mid-tide reef left), Padang Padang Right (small day option), Nyang Nyang on bigger swells
hazards: Strong shorebreak, rip currents running out to sea, submerged rocks and reef shelf especially at low tide, occasional jellyfish, sunburn on exposed white-sand beach
marine_hazards: Occasional jellyfish (especially shoulder seasons), sea urchins near rocky edges, no significant stonefish/sea snake reports; treat stings with vinegar and seawater rinse
reef_cut_severity_index: Low to medium - mostly sand with flat rock shelf; less sharp than Uluwatu/Impossibles but feet/ankle scrapes possible at very low tide
rip_current_pattern: Rip current channel typically forms between the A-frame peaks and against the cliffs on either end of the bay; identifiable by darker water with foam moving seaward; swim/paddle parallel to shore to escape
lineup_etiquette: Mixed crowd of learners and locals - extra patience required; respect priority on takeoff; beginners should stay on the inside; avoid the deeper outside line if not capable
localism_intensity: Low - very tourist-friendly atmosphere; lots of surf schools operate here so locals are accustomed to learners
respect_protocol: Be courteous to surf school students, don't snake learners, support local warungs and parking attendants with tips
resident_local_legends: No iconic local pro associated specifically with Dreamland; many Bukit-area surfers warm up here on small days [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Respect Balinese ceremonies (canang sari offerings on sand - don't step on); Nyepi (Day of Silence in March) - beach closed; cover up when leaving the beach in residential areas
medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu (closest, 24/7 with surf injury expertise), BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua, Siloam Hospital Denpasar
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu ~2-3 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~10 km; Siloam Denpasar ~18 km
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski; informal lifeguard / Balawista lifeguard presence in high season (red-yellow flag area sometimes set up) [uncertain]
emergency_contact: Indonesia emergency: 112; Bali Search and Rescue (Basarnas): +62 361 9351111; Balawista lifeguard: +62 361 755660 [uncertain]
nearest_surf_school: Dreamland Surf School (on the beach), Rip Curl School of Surf (Dreamland branch), Odyssey Surf School, Bali Green Surf School, Pro Surf School
board_rental_availability: Widely available on the beach - softboards, foam longboards, mid-lengths and shortboards from multiple stalls
surf_photographer_available: Yes - freelance land photographers on the beach and cliff; some water photographers during peak season
photographer_recommendation: Beach-based freelancers offer photo packages IDR 150,000-300,000; advance booking via Instagram common [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Klapa Resort, New Kuta Resort, Mamo Hotel, Dreamland Suites, numerous villas and homestays in Pecatu; Uluwatu/Bingin lodgings nearby
webcam_url: https://balicams.com/camera/dreamland ; surf-forecast.com webcam feeds [uncertain - status of live cams may change]
drone_allowed: Generally permitted for personal use; avoid hovering over crowds and Klapa beach club; commercial filming requires permits
coworking_nearby: Outpost Ulu, Drifter Surf Cafe (Uluwatu), Suka Espresso - all ~10-15 min by scooter
avg_lesson_price: ~IDR 550,000 (USD ~35-40) for 2-hour group lesson; private lessons IDR 700,000-1,200,000
water_quality_rating: Good in dry season - clear turquoise water; can be murkier in wet season due to runoff from the New Kuta/cliff drainage
annual_competition_window: Hosts occasional regional and grom contests; not a primary WSL venue [uncertain]
community_events: Sunset beach gatherings, Klapa beach club events, occasional Balinese ceremonies on the sand; no formal weekly surf community ritual like Single Fin Sunday
